notes: The crash in HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle (exception 0x80000004) is caused by Windows STA thread re-entrancy. On Windows, .NET's ReaderWriterLockSlim is alertable and can pump the Windows message loop during lock waits, causing re-entrant calls into HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle which corrupts state. This mechanism does not exist on macOS or Linux — STA threading and Windows message pumping are Windows-only concepts. Docker cannot simulate Windows STA behavior. SkiaSharp 2.80.2 lacks osx-arm64 native binaries, preventing testing the reporter's version. Tested SkiaSharp 3.119.2 (latest) on macOS ARM64 with concurrent stress tests (4 threads x 500 iterations of SKFont creation and SKSurface.Canvas access) — no crashes, but this is expected since STA re-entrancy doesn't exist on macOS. Triage identifies PR #1817 as the fix (shipped in 2.88.4+), replacing ReaderWriterLockSlim with non-alertable Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION.
blockers: Requires Windows with STA threading model — the crash is caused by alertable ReaderWriterLockSlim pumping the Windows message loop during lock waits, causing re-entrant calls into HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle. This mechanism does not exist on macOS or Linux.
SkiaSharp 2.80.2 lacks osx-arm64 native binaries and no x64 .NET runtime is available for Rosetta fallback — cannot test the reporter's exact version on macOS ARM64.
Docker cannot simulate Windows STA threading model — Windows containers are not supported on macOS.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console repro project with SkiaSharp 2.80.2 (reporter's version)
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/1674 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/1674 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.80.2
reproductionSteps.output: PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp' version '2.80.2' added. Warning NU1903: known high severity vulnerability.

filesCreated.description: Stress test for HandleDictionary.RegisterHandle with rapid SKFont/SKCanvas creation and concurrent access (4 threads x 500 iterations)

reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded with 2 warning(s) (NU1903 vulnerability warning).

reproductionSteps.description: Run repro with SkiaSharp 2.80.2 on macOS ARM64 — TypeInitializationException because 2.80.2 lacks osx-arm64 native binaries
reproductionSteps.output:
OS: Darwin 25.3.0
Arch: Arm64
SkiaSharp: 2.80.2
.NET: .NET 8.0.23

Test 1: CRASH: TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'SkiaSharp.SKTypeface' threw an exception.
Test 2: CRASH: TypeInitializationException
Test 3: CRASH: TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo' threw an exception.
Test 4: Concurrent test... Crashes: 2000
Test 5: CRASH: TypeInitializationException

reproductionSteps.description: Attempt x64 Rosetta fallback — no x64 .NET runtime installed
reproductionSteps.output: You must install .NET to run this application. Architecture: x64. Failed to resolve libhostfxr.dylib [not found]. Error code: 0x80008083

reproductionSteps.description: Create fresh project with latest stable SkiaSharp 3.119.2 and run stress test — all tests pass on macOS ARM64 (no STA re-entrancy on macOS)
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/1674-latest && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/1674-latest && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp && dotnet build && dotnet run
reproductionSteps.output:

Arch: Arm64
SkiaSharp: 3.119.2 (latest stable)
.NET: .NET 8.0.23

Test 1: SKFont created OK: Size=16
Test 2: 1000 SKFont creations completed OK
Test 3: SKSurface.Canvas access OK
Test 4: Concurrent (4 threads x 500)... Crashes: 0
Test 5: 500 text draws completed OK

SUCCESS: All tests completed without crash on latest stable
